records.ety: [OE. sory, sary, AS. sārig, fr. sār, n., sore. See Sore, n. & a. The original sense was, painful; hence, miserable, sad.]

defs: Grieved for the loss of some good; pained for some evil; feeling regret; -- now generally used to express light grief or affliction, but formerly often used to express deeper feeling.
quotes: Ye were made sorry after a godly manner.
I am sorry for thee, friend; 't is the duke's pleasure.
She entered, were he lief or sorry.
All full of chirking was this sorry place.

defs: Poor; mean; worthless; as, a sorry excuse.
quotes: Cheeks of sorry grain will serve.
Good fruit will sometimes grow on a sorry tree.
